codeReview.summary: The fix directly addresses the prior blocking failure by replacing file-existence checks with content-inspection commands in all five worker SKILL.md handoff-discipline sections, and by adding explicit language that `test -f` is insufficient evidence of context reads.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The transcript skeleton shows the worker performed reads/edits before invoking required skills, then invoked skills retroactively while still reporting `followedProcedure: true`; this suggests procedure-order enforcement may be unclear for documentation-only fixes.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: worker-transcripts.jsonl entry for session 881d116e-99c2-469b-a408-05f2e8214061 shows Edit/Execute commit steps before `Skill: mission-worker-base` and `Skill: switcher-integration-worker`, while handoff.skillFeedback.followedProcedure=true.

summary: Reviewed the fix transcript skeleton plus both relevant diffs (failed feature commit d698da9bccf3b2140957d5b4935b9dd7977216a7 and fix commit 178c389b797b177edc4e29ca70a123513ccd2843). The original failure was that guidance used `test -f` file-presence checks as read evidence; the fix updates all affected SKILL.md files to content-read examples (`head`/`grep`) and explicitly documents why existence checks are non-compliant, so the failure is adequately addressed.
